| 061 | 2026-05-29 RAID cycle 17 (L4.A follow-up — PgEventStore live smoke) | **D-EVENT-STORE-LIVE-SMOKE** — Cycle 17 ships `crates/dp-kernel/src/event_store_pg.rs::PgEventStore` (Q-L4A-1 WRAPPED PgPool) + `tests/integration_event_store.rs` gated by `LOREWEAVE_TEST_PG_URL`. The full `shared_test_suite::run_event_store_tests` runs against `InMemoryEventStore` in cycle-17 unit tests (proves contract soundness) but the Postgres variant only runs when the env var is set. Live smoke needs: (1) CI workflow appends `LOREWEAVE_TEST_PG_URL=postgres://lw:lw@localhost:55432/dp_kernel_test` env to cargo invocation; (2) docker-compose service in `infra/foundation-dev/docker-compose.yml` named `dp-kernel-test-pg` (separate DB from foundation pg so per-reality migration 0002+0004 can be applied without polluting dev data); (3) `scripts/raid/test-infra-up-dps.sh 17 1` extension that brings up the test DB + runs `migrate up` on it; (4) gate `cargo test -p dp-kernel --test integration_event_store` in CI's verify-cycle-17 step. Same shape as cycle-16 D-EMBEDDING-QUEUE-LIVE-WIRING. | RAID cycle 33 (L7 ops integration) or earlier opportunistic CI hardening | MED |
| 060 | 2026-05-29 RAID cycle 16 (L3.I follow-up — V1+30d Redis-backed queue per Q-L3-1) | **D-EMBEDDING-QUEUE-REDIS-MIGRATION** — Cycle 16 ships the embedding queue as an in-process `VecDeque<MemoryRef>` inside `world-service`. Q-L3-1 LOCKED says "V1 in world-service async queue; V1+30d extract if embedding volume justifies". The V1+30d trigger is volume > N realities × M memories/min sustained (precise threshold TBD by L7 ops obs). Migration shape: keep `EmbeddingProvider`+`EmbeddingWriter`+`AuditWriter` traits intact; swap `Queue` for a Redis-backed FIFO (XADD/XREADGROUP on `lw-embedding-jobs` stream) so multi-replica world-service deployments don't double-bill the provider. New `embedding-worker` service shape mirrors archive-worker/retention-worker pattern (cycles 11). | RAID cycle 33 (L7 ops integration) or 1-month post-V1 launch | MED |
| 059 | 2026-05-29 RAID cycle 16 (L3.I follow-up — embedding queue live wiring) | **D-EMBEDDING-QUEUE-LIVE-WIRING** *(RE-TARGETED by cycle 17)* — Cycle 16 ships `services/world-service/src/embedding_queue/` as pure library code with abstract IO (`EmbeddingProvider`, `EmbeddingWriter`, `AuditWriter` traits). Real wiring needs: (1) `provider-registry-service` HTTP client adapter binding `EmbeddingProvider` to BYOK provider gateway (CLAUDE.md provider gateway invariant — NO direct vendor SDK); (2) sqlx PgPool `EmbeddingWriter` running `UPDATE npc_session_memory_embedding SET embedding=$1::vector WHERE npc_id=$2 AND session_id=$3 AND embedding IS NULL` against the per-reality DB (DSN looked up via cycle-7 reality_registry); (3) `meta-rs::MetaWrite` adapter binding `AuditWriter` to `service_to_service_audit` table per Q-L1A-3; (4) tokio task spawning + graceful shutdown; (5) `/healthz` + `lw_embedding_queue_depth` + `lw_embedding_queue_failures_total` + `lw_embedding_provider_tokens_total` Prom metrics; (6) projection-runner wire-up: cycle-12 dp-kernel `ProjectionRunner` calls `embedding_queue.enqueue(MemoryRef)` whenever `npc.memory_embedded` event lands and the row was INSERTed with `embedding=NULL`; (7) daily integrity-checker (cycle 15) extension that scans for stale-NULL embeddings (older than 1h) and re-enqueues. Cycle 17 confirmed L4.A is trait-shape + macros only (NOT integration); re-target to cycle 33 (L7 ops integration) consolidated with rows 054/057/058. | RAID cycle 33 (L7 ops integration) | HIGH |
| 058 | 2026-05-29 RAID cycle 11 (L2.K follow-up — retention-worker live wiring) | **D-RETENTION-WORKER-LIVE-WIRING** *(RE-TARGETED by cycle 17)* — Cycle 11 ships `services/retention-worker/` as pure libraries with abstract IO (Deleter, ScriptRunner, DSNLookup, ModeReader). `cmd/retention-worker/main.go` is a SKELETON. Real wiring needs (1) pgx Deleter binding the bounded-batch DELETE WHERE published=TRUE AND dead_lettered_at IS NULL AND last_attempt_at < NOW()-grace + LIMIT N; (2) os/exec ScriptRunner shelling out to `bash scripts/event-audit-retention-cron.sh --db $DSN`; (3) reality_registry DSNLookup binding; (4) hourly ticker + graceful shutdown loop; (5) /healthz + /readyz HTTP + Prom /metrics for `lw_retention_*` series. | 040 | 2026-05-28 v3.5 POST-REVIEW PO directive (geo-generator-amaw, renumbered from local 026 on merge) | **Natural-fragmentation enhancements** (umbrella entry for ~10 features). Real continents are sculpted by water + wind + tectonic break-up — not just drawn algorithmically. v3.5 fractalize ships RANDOM fractal detail; future passes add PHYSICS-DRIVEN fractal detail: (a) coastal wave erosion; (b) wind/Aeolian erosion; (c) river-mouth carving/deltas; (d) tectonic rifting (active plate break-up); (e) sea-level dynamics (glacial/interglacial floods); (f) subduction-driven asymmetric coast modification; (g) hotspot volcanic island chains; (h) karst/dissolution coast detail per-biome; (i) glacial fjord carving; (j) storm-track erosion bands. Full list documented in `docs/plans/2026-05-25-phase-a-v3-roadmap.md` §13 "Natural-fragmentation enhancements". | Phase B+ (water/wind erosion) and V5 (time dynamics) | LOW |

If composite drops >10 PO can flag specific seeds for follow-up via /review-impl. | v4.5 calibration phase | MED |

| 037 | 2026-05-24 V1 tilemap-viewer rescope | Foundation tiles are now algorithm-generated (FFT-tileable noise + per-terrain palette + small specials per `frontend-game/scripts/gen-foundation-tiles.py`) — license-clean + seamless tileable, replaces the prior HoMM3-Flux1d placeholders. V2 still needs an AI-gen pipeline for the **prop/decoration overlay layer** (trees, structures, mushrooms placed on top of the foundation) — that layer is what the multi-book platform invests in for art polish. Front-view portrait sprites + hand-painted encounter backgrounds also belong here (separate branches). Multi-book constraint: per-book prop gen must complete in <few hours at low cost; license-clean (schnell / SDXL / CC0). | V2 asset pipeline (prop/overlay layer) | MED |
| 036 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | `frontend-game/src/game/main.ts` ships a `globalThis.Phaser = Phaser` shim required because Phaser 4.1.0's webpack-bundled ESM references the bare global `Phaser` identifier internally (`phaser.esm.js:88604` `new Phaser.Structs.Map()` inside SpriteGPULayer constructor; lines 33884/34948/34969 in render code). Without the shim, calling SpriteGPULayer factory throws "Phaser is not defined" at runtime. Track upstream — remove shim once Phaser ships ESM-clean patch (likely 4.x.y). Saved to memory `project_phaser4_quirks`. | When Phaser 4 ships ESM-clean patch | LOW |
| 035 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | game-server EchoRoom has no rate limiting — a malicious client could spam-send messages. V0 acceptable because EchoRoom is dev-only smoke; V1 ZoneRoom/CombatRoom must add per-client rate limits (Colyseus supports via `client.send` budget or middleware) before any production deploy. | V1 game-server rooms | MED |
| 034 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | CORS origins in tilemap-service + game-server default to `http://localhost:5174` via `LOREWEAVE_CORS_ORIGINS` env. Production deploy MUST set explicit origin(s) (not `*`); if env is unset or empty in prod, the cors layer falls back to localhost-only which would block real users. Add a startup check in V1 prod build that env is non-empty when `NODE_ENV=production`. | V1 prod deploy hardening | MED |
| 033 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | `frontend-game/src/config/services.ts` `SERVICES.devToken` defaults to `'dev_internal_token'` which bundles into client JS (visible in DevTools). V0 dev-only token is acceptable for echo-room handshake; V1 MUST replace with per-user JWT obtained via auth-service login flow + cookie storage (HTTP-only, SameSite=Lax). Currently EchoPanel imports + sends this token to game-server EchoRoom.onAuth. Anyone who can reach localhost:2567 can join with the leaked token. | V1 auth-service integration | MED |
| 032 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | EchoRoom reconnect uses Colyseus `setSeatReservationTime(30)` (30-second window). Docker container restart often takes >30s → reconnect-token reservation expires → client falls back to fresh `joinOrCreate` (correct UX per EchoPanel `onReconnect` catch handler). When V1 real ZoneRoom ships with persistent character state, reconsider whether 30s window is right or if a longer reservation (e.g. 5 min) matches user expectation. | V1 ZoneRoom design | LOW |
| 031 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | TilemapGPULayer documented as N/A for our iso 2:1 dimetric (spec §11.1 amended Session C Phase 1; Phaser docs `CHANGELOG-v4.0.0.md:549` confirm orthographic-only). If we ever scale Continent zone from 256² with standard `Tilemap.createLayer` and need GPU-layer perf, the only path is either (a) reproject world as orthographic (visual style change) or (b) hand-roll a custom GPU tilemap shader (V3+). Not blocking V1; current path handles V0+V1 zone sizes fine. | V3+ continent perf | LOW |

| 028 | 2026-05-18 continent measurement /review-impl LOW-3 | `tilemap-service measure` always runs the offline 256² placement (~8–11 min) before the live engine→L3→L4 section. Re-running it purely to capture the live numbers (e.g. after the provider-registry model-pricing fix) re-pays that ~11-min cost for nothing. Add a `measure --live-only` flag, or serialise/cache the placed `TilemapView` so the live section can load it. | tilemap measurement ergonomics (Track 2) | LOW |
| 025 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E spec §5.7 | `RoadPlacer` ships raw Dijkstra paths — no §3.4-step-3 path smoothing (3-point detour removal) and a single road kind (no `RoadKind` Highway/DirtPath). V1+30d has no town/mine edges, so every MST edge is zone-backbone — one kind suffices; smoothing is pure visual polish. Build when rendered roads look jagged or a town/mine tier needs a secondary road kind. | Visual polish (Track 2) | LOW |
| 024 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D spec D-Q6 | TMP_007 §5's "curved" passage-path cost — a `search_path` cost that makes a passage corridor bow away from the zone border instead of bisecting the zone — is not built. Phase D drives `search_path` with uniform `cost = 1.0` (the shortest route); the curved cost is pure visual polish with no correctness or determinism role. Build it when rendered passage corridors look too straight / zone-bisecting. | Visual polish (Track 2) | LOW |
| 023 | 2026-05-17 tilemap Phase C human-in-loop review (Call #1) | TMP_006 §3.2 zone-config object **filtering** — `banned_object_categories`, `banned_objects`, `required_objects` — is not built in Phase C. It is a separate object-pool-filtering layer needing its own `ZoneSpec` fields (`banned_objects: BTreeSet<…>`, `required_objects: BTreeMap<…>`, …) and a filter pass over the treasure pool; no TMP_006 §8.5 resolved question makes it V1+30d-mandatory — unlike `inherit_treasure_from` (TMP-TR-Q3), which Phase C *does* build (spec D9). The A–E modificator roadmap (Phase D = ConnectionsPlacer, E = Roads/Rivers) has no slot for authoring-layer features, so this is logged with an explicit target rather than left to drift. **Also reconcile here:** Phase C spec D9 chose REPLACE over TMP-TR-Q3's UNION-with-override for the unkeyed `treasure_tiers` `Vec`; when this keyed filtering layer lands, define the per-key override semantics for `banned_objects` / `required_objects` consistently with TMP-TR-Q3. | TMP_004 authoring task (Track 2) | LOW |
| 021 | 2026-05-17 tilemap Phase A spec D12 / code-review r3 WARN-3 | `From<PlacementError> for crate::Error` bridge not built. Phase A's `place_and_connect_object` returns the module-local `PlacementError` enum (`NoSpace` / `NoSuchZone`); a placer in Phase C+ that `?`-propagates from `Modificator::process` (returns `crate::Result`) needs the conversion. Phase A has no such consumer, so the bridge is deferred to the first placer. Spec D12 records this. | Phase C (first placer) | LOW |
| 020 | 2026-05-17 tilemap Phase A spec D5 | TMP_006 §4.3 connectivity pre-filter not built — `would_seal_a_gap` runs the naive double-flood-fill on every candidate placement. TMP_006 §4.2 blesses that as "cheap enough to run on every candidate"; the §4.3 pre-filter (skip candidates adjacent to ≤1 free region) is a continent-scale perf optimisation. Output stays byte-identical. | Perf — when continent-scale generation profiles slow | LOW |
| 018 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 human-in-loop review HIGH-1 | `penrose::assign_zone_tiles` is O(tiles × vertices) — `nearest_vertex` linearly scans the full Penrose vertex field for every grid tile. At Continent scale (256² = 65 536 tiles × ~500-1200 vertices) that is ~10⁷-10⁸ `distance_sq` calls, single-threaded, vs the TMP_002 §7 budget. Structurally the same defect as #016 (fractalize) but larger — the tile loop runs over the whole grid, not one zone — and the capped AMAW Adversary caught only #016. Invisible at the 24²-64² test fixtures. Fix: bucket vertices into a coarse uniform grid and test only neighbour buckets, or a multi-source BFS distance transform — output stays identical. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256² `place_tilemap` takes ~8–11 min release-build — recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf — **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |
| 017 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-3 | `force_directed_converge` computes `ConvergenceResult.converged` + `.iteration_count`, but `place_zones` / `place_tilemap` drop them — `TilemapView` has no carrier, so a cap-hit tilemap is indistinguishable from a cleanly-converged one. PLAN chunk 0 said "add `iteration_count`/`converged` carriers as needed"; nothing in Phase 1 consumes them. Surface via a `GenerationMetadata` field on `TilemapView` when DP progress-streaming (TMP_002 §9 TMP-PLACE-Q3) lands. | Phase 2+ (progress-event streaming) | LOW |
| 016 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-2 | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` is O(candidates²) — `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linearly scans the growing `cleared` mask for every candidate. At Continent scale (256²) that is ~10⁸ ops per zone vs the TMP_002 §7 <500 ms budget; invisible at the 24²-64² test fixtures. Replace the linear nearest-scan with coarse spatial buckets or a multi-source BFS distance transform. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256² `place_tilemap` takes ~8–11 min release-build — recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf — **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |
| 015 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-1 (severity raised by human-in-loop review) | The wall-clock-cap fallback (`force_directed` D5) is a cross-machine layout divergence: a slow machine can trip the wall-clock cap where a fast one converges, so the grid-seed fallback differs across machines for the same seed — the one path that can break the TMP-A4 axiom. Spec-D5-accepted. **A `tracing::warn!` in the `WallClockCap` arm now makes the fallback observable** (added 2026-05-16 review-fix task). The remaining deferred part is the ops-dashboard `tilemap.generation_failed` info-event (TMP-PLACE-Q2), which needs the Phase-2 progress-event channel. | Phase 2+ (progress-event streaming) | MED |
| 014 | 2026-05-16 AMAW-improve BUILD finding | `amaw-guardrail-gate.py` cheap pre-check matches a risky pattern ANYWHERE in the Bash command string — including a heredoc body / commit message. A `git commit` whose message text contains "git push" or "migrat…" false-positive-fires the gate (an `ask`, not a block — so non-fatal, the user just confirms). Refine: match the leading command token, or strip heredoc bodies before the pre-check. | AMAW hook hardening | LOW |
| 013 | 2026-05-16 AMAW-improve spec D3 | `agentic-workflow/AMAW.md` (the installable bundle source of `docs/amaw-workflow.md`) was already ~67 lines diverged from the installed copy before this task; D3 updated the operative installed docs (`amaw-workflow.md` + `amaw.md`×2) but not `AMAW.md`. Reconciling the bundle source is its own hygiene task. | agentic-workflow bundle sync | LOW |
| 010 | 2026-05-15 tilemap Phase 0b spec §2 | Anthropic **request-side** tool support — `anthropicAdapter.Stream` sends no `tools`/`tool_choice` (Anthropic-shaped tools differ from the OpenAI-shaped contract field). Phase 0b landed only the Anthropic *streamer parse-side* `tool_use`→`tool_call` mapping. Fix when an Anthropic provider needs tool-use: translate OpenAI-shaped `tools`/`tool_choice` → Anthropic `input_schema`/`tool_choice` in `anthropicAdapter.Stream`, and set `anthropicAdapter.SupportsTools()` → true. | When Anthropic tool-use needed | MED |
| 009 | 2026-05-15 tilemap Phase 0b code review (Scope Guard AC-7 PARTIAL) | D8 guard (`LLM_TOOLS_NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_PROVIDER`) full handler-level reject path is untested — the capability component (`SupportsTools()` + `hasToolDefinitions()`) is unit-tested, but the `doLlmStream` reject path needs the live-DB + fake-adapter integration harness. Joins the pre-existing `D-PHASE5A-STREAM-INTEGRATION-TESTS` deferral. | provider-registry stream integration suite | LOW |
| 008 | 2026-05-15 amaw-precommit-no-side-effect Adversary r1 WARN-1 | `load_state()` calls `json.loads()` with no try/except — a corrupt or empty `.workflow-state.json` (manual edit, disk corruption) makes every gate command, incl. `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, die with a traceback that blocks the commit. (Risk much reduced by DEFERRED #003's atomic save_state — crashes can no longer half-write the file — but manual-edit / disk-corruption paths remain.) Fix: wrap `load_state`'s parse in try/except; on corruption, print a clear error and either reset or refuse cleanly. Affects all `load_state` callers, so it is its own task — not in #004's scope. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 007 | 2026-05-15 amaw-task-slug-validation Adversary r2 WARN-1 | `cmd_amaw_enable` normalizes the slug only on the `if args:` write path; a no-arg or already-enabled invocation can leave a raw `state["task"]` persisted in the state file. Mitigated: `cmd_complete` defensively re-normalizes so the OUTPUT tag list is always comma-safe — only the state-file display value can be stale. Fix: normalize unconditionally at the top of `cmd_amaw_enable`, or normalize on every state read. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 001 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #5 | `task_slug` not validated — comma in slug splits into multiple tags downstream (`",".join(tags)` then mcp-query.py splits back on comma). No injection risk (subprocess list form). Fix: normalize comma → dash in `cmd_amaw_enable`, or argparse-validate slug pattern `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 002 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #6 | `"REJECTED" in evidence.upper()` substring match in `cmd_complete` bridge logic — false positives on "NOT REJECTED" or "rejected the rejected pattern". Fix: word-boundary regex `\bREJECTED\b` or check evidence prefix `"REJECTED:"` / `"status: REJECTED"`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 004 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #8 | `cmd_pre_commit` early-exits silent when no STATE_FILE, but `&&`-chained `cmd_amaw_pre_commit` then calls `load_state()` which CREATES `.workflow-state.json` from INITIAL_STATE. Side effect: every commit by an agent NOT in any tracked task creates a stale state file, surfacing confusing `[ ]` markers on next `status`. Fix: in `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, also early-exit if STATE_FILE absent before `load_state()`. | L4 hardening | LOW |

## Recently cleared (within 5 sessions)

| ID | Cleared | How |
|---|---|---|
| 038 | 2026-05-24 V1 tilemap-viewer rescope (same session it was created) | HoMM3-bundle Flux1-dev placeholders replaced with first-party algorithm-generated foundation tiles (`frontend-game/scripts/gen-foundation-tiles.py`). No third-party model output baked in; ship-anywhere license. Spec rescope: `docs/specs/2026-05-24-v1-tilemap-viewer-rescope.md`. |
| 024 | 2026-05-24 v2.1e ship (geo-generator-amaw branch) | W9 shipped as amplitude-gated RGB shading (zone relief auto-scales intensity 0→1 across Plains→Mountains; ±15% RGB modulation). Hypothesis "naive RGB regressed because eval framework had a problem" CONFIRMED: under v4.3 ecotone-aware eval, naive RGB amplitude-gated produces only −0.24 mean (neutral, 0 hard regressions) vs the v2.1b attempt's hard regression. Visual benefit currently limited (visible only on Ice zones where white amplifies modulation) but ships as foundation for future 3D map rendering. HSL approach not needed. True slope-aware hillshade (spec option B) still deferred to v3+. |
| 027 | 2026-05-22 tilemap test hardening | Added `a_bridge_mid_river_resets_the_periodic_ford_counter` (river_placer.rs): a 30×3 straight river with a road at x=6; asserts the bridge resets the shared `since_crossing` counter, so the periodic ford lands at x=18 (12 carves after the bridge) — NOT x=12 (which would mean the bridge didn't reset). Closes the interleaving coverage gap between `a_long_river_gets_periodic_fords` (pure every-Nth) and `a_river_crossing_a_road...` (pure bridge). Test-only; no output change. |
| 026 | 2026-05-22 tilemap river barrier reorder | Root cause refined: **erosion** (not fill) fragments the passable region, and the river's Mountain/Lake sources/sinks required erosion first — so the river was structurally stuck threading post-erosion narrow channels (forded most tiles). Fix: split `ObstaclePlacer` into `ObstacleSourcePlacer` (places Mountain/Lake markers on Open tiles **pre-erosion**, dual-gated per-zone + map-wide) → `RiverPlacer` (carves the wide-open zone) → `ObstacleFillPlacer` (erode + fill the rest post-river, skipping river Water; erosion also skips Water fords). Golden fixture river ford ratio: **~0.77 crossings → 0.06 fords** (AC-2 gates < 0.25). Cost tradeoff accepted: continent 6.46 s → 10.56 s (river now carves a real barrier). Strict dual `would_seal_a_gap` gate unchanged. `/review-impl` caught a HIGH (source placer needed the map-wide gate arm, not per-zone only) — fixed + regression-tested. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md`](../specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md). |
| 029 | 2026-05-21 tilemap perf fix | `place_and_connect_object` refactored to **score-first, validate-on-demand**: cheap-filter + score all candidates (no flood fill), sort by `(score desc, flat asc)`, walk in best-first order and validate (`would_seal_a_gap` + `find_access_path`) lazily. The first sorted candidate that passes IS the winner (spec §4 proof: `first ∈ V` of sorted `S` == `argmax_{v ∈ V} (score, -flat)`). Naive kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as oracle. **Measurement: `treasure_placer` 973.376 s → 2.944 s (330×); continent total 992.963 s → 21.541 s (46×).** Golden test passes byte-exact — no rebaseline. AC-1 oracle: 405 agreements across 3 fixtures × 3 templates × 3 optimisations × 3 min-distances × 5 sequential placements. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md`](../specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md). |
| 018 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `penrose::nearest_vertex` replaced with a bucket-based spiral search (`build_vertex_buckets` + `nearest_vertex_bucketed` over the new `UniformBuckets<Vec2>` helper in `engine/placement/spatial.rs`). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior linear scan (AC-2 oracle: 800 random queries × 4 vertex-field configurations incl. 1500-vertex dense; AC-3 cross-bucket tie-break by lowest index). Per-stage continent measurement: `place_zones` (Penrose + fractalize) dropped to **0.110 s** at 256² — well under the TMP_002 §7 <500 ms budget. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 016 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` replaced with a bucket-based "any within radius" rewrite using the same `UniformBuckets<TileCoord>` helper — 3×3 ring neighbourhood query (sufficiency proven: `bucket_size = ceil(sqrt(coverage_sq))` ⇒ ring-2 min dist² ≥ 49 > 36 surface / 25 > 16 sea). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linear scan (AC-1 oracle: 96² zone × 5 seeds × 2 spans). The naive impl is kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as the oracle. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 022 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E | Resolved by the Phase-E `RiverPlacer` design (spec D-Q5/D-Q6): the `anchor` representative point **suffices** — no footprint extent needed. River sources are one lowest-flat-index `Mountain` obstacle anchor per mountain-bearing zone; sinks are the `Lake` obstacle anchors ∪ `Sea`-zone tiles. `search_path` routes to/from the anchor tile, which is added to the river search area. No additive `TilemapObjectPlacement` footprint field was required. |
| 019 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D | Re-validated when ConnectionsPlacer landed. `seal_borders` (§3.1/§9) seals inter-zone borders but is not a complete wall — passage corridors stay `Walkable` and gap-critical tiles stay `Open`. The per-zone `would_seal_a_gap` is sound regardless: every tile belongs to exactly one zone, and every passability-removing mutation (placer objects, monoliths, guards, ferries, border seals) targets an `Open` tile that is zone-interior — so removing a zone-X tile can never split zone Y. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |
| 013 | 2026-05-17 GEO Phase 2 | Connectivity-aware `choose_sea_level` — binary-searches `sea_level` so the largest land component ≈ the profile target (`maxcomp(L(s))` is monotone non-increasing in `s`), plus a continental base dome for the high-land `Inland` profile (a blob-only heightmap cannot form a connected 70% landmass). Realised land fraction is now within ±0.08 of target, or a documented 8192 best-effort floor (±0.18). Verified by `land_fraction_near_target` (16-seed sweep × 4 profiles). |
| 012 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Added `TestStreamOpenAICompat_MultipleToolCalls` — two tool calls streamed with interleaved/non-monotonic `tool_calls[].index` (0,1,1,0); asserts `Index` is verbatim from the provider and per-index reassembly stays separate. |
| 011 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Dropped `nullable: true` from openapi `ChatStreamRequest.tool_choice` — the field is now `description` + `oneOf` only (idiomatic OpenAPI 3.0; still optional via not-in-`required`). No runtime change; SDKs already model it optional. |
| 005 | 2026-05-15 tilemap Phase 0b /amaw | First full-feature `/amaw` dogfood (XL, 12 phases). 6 sub-agent spawns (4 design Adversary + 1 code Adversary + 1 Scope Guard) all completed their Step-0 `python scripts/mcp-query.py` calls cleanly — no permission-prompt interruption. The allow-list is sufficient. |
| 003 | 2026-05-15 amaw-atomic-save-state | First `/amaw` dogfood. `save_state` now writes a PID-unique `.tmp` then `Path.replace()` (atomic rename); `cmd_reset` sweeps stale tmps; `finally`-unlink cleans own tmp. Adversary r1 added 3 hardening WARNs (all fixed). |
| 006 | 2026-05-15 batch pre-flight | AMAW.md Adversary + Scope Guard prompt templates now pre-instruct the Bash-heredoc fallback for writing findings/post-review docs, since the Write tool blocks sub-agents from writing report files. |
